seoTitle: Silicontent Technology Power Management IC Products | DC-DC LDO | BeiLuo Electronics
seoDescription: Browse Silicontent Technology power management ICs including DC-DC converters, LDO regulators, and power modules. High-efficiency solutions for consumer, industrial, and automotive applications.

categories.name: DC-DC Converters
categories.description: High-efficiency buck, boost, and buck-boost converters for point-of-load power conversion
categories.longDescription: Silicontent DC-DC converters deliver industry-leading power density and efficiency for space-constrained applications. Products include synchronous buck converters with switching frequencies up to 3MHz, enabling small external inductors and capacitors. Wide input voltage ranges, fast transient response, and comprehensive protection features make them ideal for smartphones, IoT devices, and industrial equipment. As an authorized Silicontent distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive selection guide and technical support for choosing the right DC-DC converter for your application.

series.name: XZ2xxx Series
series.description: High-efficiency synchronous buck converters up to 3A output

series.name: XZ3xxx Series
series.description: Ultra-high frequency buck converters for space-constrained designs

categories.selectionGuide: Choose XZ2xxx series for high-current applications up to 3A. Select XZ3xxx series for ultra-compact designs requiring small external components. Consider input voltage range, output current, and switching frequency based on your application requirements.

products.name: XZ2105 3A Synchronous Buck Converter
products.shortDescription: High-efficiency 3A synchronous buck converter with 2.5MHz switching frequency for compact designs
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ2105 is a high-efficiency synchronous buck converter capable of delivering up to 3A continuous output current. With a switching frequency of 2.5MHz, it enables the use of small external inductors and capacitors, reducing overall solution size.

The device operates from a 2.5V to 5.5V input voltage range, making it ideal for single Li-ion battery-powered applications. It features internal compensation, fast transient response, and excellent load regulation. The peak current mode control architecture provides fast transient response with minimal output voltage ripple.

Comprehensive protection features include cycle-by-cycle current limit, thermal shutdown, and undervoltage lockout. The XZ2105 is available in a compact 2mm x 2mm QFN package, making it perfect for space-constrained applications such as smartphones, tablets, and portable devices.
features: 2.5MHz switching frequency for small external components

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Consumer Electronics
faeReview.content: The XZ2105 is an excellent buck converter that I've used in several smartphone power designs. The 2.5MHz switching frequency really makes a difference - you can use a 1μH inductor in a small 2mm x 1.6mm package, saving significant board space. Efficiency is impressive, consistently achieving 93-95% in typical operating conditions. The internal compensation simplifies design - no need to calculate external compensation networks. Transient response is fast enough for most processor loads, though for the most demanding applications you might want to add extra output capacitance. One thing I appreciate is the clean switching waveform with minimal ringing, which helps with EMI. The power-good indicator is useful for sequencing multiple rails. Overall, a solid choice for 3A applications where space is at a premium.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent 3A buck converter with 2.5MHz frequency for compact designs

alternativeParts.reason: Industry-standard 3A buck converter with similar performance characteristics
alternativeParts.useCase: Alternative for designs requiring TI compatibility

alternativeParts.reason: Comparable 3A buck converter from MPS with similar features

companionParts.description: Ultra-low noise LDO for sensitive analog circuits
Integrated power module with XZ2105 and inductor
Li-ion battery charger for single-cell applications

faqs.question: What is the maximum output current of XZ2105?
faqs.answer: The XZ2105 can deliver up to 3A continuous output current with proper thermal management. The actual maximum current may be limited by thermal considerations depending on ambient temperature and PCB design. Peak current capability is higher for transient loads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for 20-30% current margin below maximum rating for reliable operation; ensure adequate PCB copper area for heat dissipation.

faqs.question: What inductor value should I use with XZ2105?
faqs.answer: For XZ2105's 2.5MHz switching frequency, a 1.0μH to 1.5μH inductor is recommended. Lower inductance values enable faster transient response but result in higher ripple current. Use inductors with saturation current rating at least 1.3 times the maximum load current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with 1.0μH for typical applications; use 0.47μH for fastest transient response or 2.2μH for lowest ripple.

faqs.question: How do I set the output voltage?
faqs.answer: Output voltage is set using a resistive divider from VOUT to FB pin to GND. The feedback reference voltage is 0.6V. Calculate R1 (top resistor) and R2 (bottom resistor) using: VOUT = 0.6V × (1 + R1/R2). Use 1% tolerance resistors for accurate output voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose resistor values in the 10kΩ to 1MΩ range; lower values improve noise immunity but increase quiescent current.

faqs.question: What input and output capacitors are recommended?
faqs.answer: For input, use 10μF to 22μF ceramic capacitors with X5R or X7R dielectric rated for the input voltage. For output, use 22μF to 47μF ceramic capacitors. Low ESR is important for stability and ripple performance. Place capacitors close to the IC pins.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use ceramic capacitors with X7R dielectric for best temperature stability; add electrolytic capacitor for additional bulk capacitance if needed.

faqs.question: How do I use the power-good (PG) output?
faqs.answer: The PG output is an open-drain signal that goes high impedance when the output voltage is within regulation. Connect a 100kΩ pull-up resistor to a logic voltage (1.8V, 3.3V, or 5V). PG can be used for power sequencing, reset generation, or monitoring.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use PG for power sequencing in multi-rail systems; connect to processor reset input for power-on reset generation.

products.name: XZ3102 2A Ultra-High Frequency Buck Converter
products.shortDescription: Compact 2A buck converter with 3MHz switching frequency for ultra-small solution size
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ3102 is an ultra-high frequency synchronous buck converter designed for space-constrained applications. With a switching frequency of 3MHz, it enables the use of tiny chip inductors as small as 1.0mm x 1.0mm, minimizing overall solution size.

Operating from a 2.3V to 5.5V input, the XZ3102 delivers up to 2A output current with excellent efficiency across the load range. The device features automatic power-save mode for high efficiency at light loads, seamlessly transitioning to PWM mode for heavy loads.

Advanced features include programmable soft-start, input undervoltage lockout, thermal shutdown, and short-circuit protection. The XZ3102 is available in an ultra-small 1.5mm x 1.5mm WLCSP package, making it ideal for wearable devices and ultra-compact electronics.
features: 2A output current in compact form factor
Automatic power-save mode for light load efficiency

faeReview.author: Sarah Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Wearable Electronics
faeReview.content: The XZ3102 is perfect for wearable applications where every fraction of a millimeter counts. The 3MHz frequency allows use of 0402-size inductors, and the WLCSP package is incredibly small. I've used this in smartwatch designs where PCB area is extremely limited. The automatic power-save mode works well - efficiency stays above 85% even at very light loads, which is critical for battery life. The trade-off is slightly lower peak efficiency compared to lower-frequency converters, but for wearables, the size savings are worth it. One tip: the WLCSP package requires careful PCB design - follow the layout guidelines closely for proper solder joint reliability. Also, the higher switching frequency means more attention to input capacitor placement to minimize EMI.
faeReview.highlight: Ultra-compact 2A converter with 3MHz frequency for wearable designs

alternativeParts.reason: Similar ultra-high frequency buck converter with comparable specs
alternativeParts.useCase: Alternative for TI-based designs requiring similar performance

alternativeParts.reason: Comparable compact buck converter from MPS

companionParts.description: Higher current 3A option from same family
Compact battery charger for wearable applications

faqs.question: What makes XZ3102 suitable for wearable applications?
faqs.answer: XZ3102 features an ultra-small 1.5mm x 1.5mm WLCSP package and 3MHz switching frequency that enables tiny external components. The automatic power-save mode maintains high efficiency at light loads typical of wearable devices. Low quiescent current (<20μA) minimizes battery drain during standby.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use XZ3102 for space-constrained wearable designs; consider XZ2105 for higher current requirements.

faqs.question: What inductor size can be used with XZ3102?
faqs.answer: At 3MHz switching frequency, XZ3102 can use chip inductors as small as 0402 (1.0mm x 1.0mm) with values of 0.47μH to 1.0μH. This enables the smallest possible solution size for space-constrained applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 0.47μH chip inductor for smallest size; verify inductor saturation current rating exceeds maximum load current.

faqs.question: How does the power-save mode work?
faqs.answer: XZ3102 automatically enters power-save mode at light loads, operating in PFM (pulse frequency modulation) to minimize switching losses. As load increases, it seamlessly transitions to PWM mode for full-frequency operation. This maintains high efficiency across the entire load range.
faqs.decisionGuide: Power-save mode is automatic; no external configuration needed. Light load efficiency is optimized for battery-powered applications.

faqs.question: What PCB layout considerations are important for XZ3102?
faqs.answer: Due to the high switching frequency and small package, careful PCB layout is essential. Keep input capacitor close to VIN and GND pins, minimize the switching node (SW) trace length, and provide adequate ground plane. Follow the evaluation board layout as a reference.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 4-layer PCB if possible for better EMI performance; follow manufacturer layout guidelines closely.

faqs.question: Can XZ3102 be used in multi-rail power systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, XZ3102 can be synchronized to an external clock for multi-rail systems requiring precise switching frequency alignment. This prevents beat frequencies and simplifies EMI filtering in systems with multiple switching regulators.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SYNC feature for multi-rail systems; connect all converters to common clock source for frequency synchronization.

products.name: XZ2201 1A Synchronous Buck Converter
products.shortDescription: Compact 1A synchronous buck converter with 1.5MHz switching frequency for portable applications
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ2201 is a compact synchronous buck converter designed for portable and battery-powered applications. With 1A output current capability and 1.5MHz switching frequency, it provides an optimal balance of efficiency and solution size.

Operating from a 2.5V to 5.5V input voltage range, the XZ2201 is ideal for single Li-ion battery applications. It features automatic power-save mode for high efficiency at light loads and comprehensive protection features.
The device is available in a small SOT-23-5 package, making it perfect for space-constrained designs such as wearables, IoT devices, and portable electronics.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Consumer Electronics
faeReview.content: The XZ2201 is an excellent choice for low-current applications where simplicity and cost are important. The SOT-23-5 package is easy to work with and widely available. I've used this part in several IoT sensor designs where the 1A capability is more than sufficient. The 1.5MHz frequency allows use of a 2.2μH inductor, which is a good balance between size and efficiency. The automatic power-save mode works well - efficiency stays above 85% even at very light loads. One advantage of the lower frequency compared to the XZ3102 is slightly better peak efficiency. Overall, a solid, reliable buck converter for cost-sensitive applications.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-effective 1A buck converter in easy-to-use SOT-23-5 package

alternativeParts.reason: Industry-standard 1A buck converter

faqs.question: What inductor value should I use with XZ2201?
faqs.answer: For XZ2201's 1.5MHz switching frequency, a 2.2μH inductor is recommended. This provides a good balance between ripple current and solution size. Use inductors with saturation current rating at least 1.3 times the maximum load current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 2.2μH for typical applications; 1.5μH for smaller size or 3.3μH for lower ripple.

products.name: XZ2301 4A High-Current Buck Converter
products.shortDescription: High-current 4A synchronous buck converter with 1.2MHz switching for industrial and computing applications
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ2301 is a high-current synchronous buck converter capable of delivering up to 4A continuous output current. With a switching frequency of 1.2MHz, it provides high efficiency for demanding applications.

The device operates from a 4.5V to 18V input voltage range, making it suitable for industrial, computing, and networking applications. It features external compensation for optimized transient response and supports programmable soft-start.

Comprehensive protection features include cycle-by-cycle current limit, thermal shutdown, and undervoltage lockout. The XZ2301 is available in a thermally enhanced QFN-16 package for high-power applications.

faeReview.author: Robert Li
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Systems
faeReview.content: The XZ2301 is our go-to solution for high-current applications. The 4A capability and wide input range make it very versatile for industrial and computing applications. I've used this in several FPGA power designs where the 4A capacity handles the peak currents during configuration. The external compensation allows optimization for specific transient requirements - this is important for processors with aggressive power management. The 1.2MHz switching provides good efficiency while keeping external components reasonable in size. Thermal management is important at 4A - use adequate copper area and thermal vias. Overall, a robust high-current buck converter with excellent performance.
faeReview.highlight: High-current 4A buck converter with wide input range for industrial applications

alternativeParts.reason: Industry-standard 4A buck converter

faqs.question: What is the maximum output current of XZ2301?
faqs.answer: The XZ2301 can deliver up to 4A continuous output current with proper thermal management. The actual maximum current may be limited by thermal considerations depending on ambient temperature and PCB design. Peak current capability is higher for transient loads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for 20-30% current margin below maximum rating; ensure adequate PCB copper area and thermal vias for heat dissipation.

faqs.question: How do I select between XZ2xxx and XZ3xxx series?
faqs.answer: Choose XZ2xxx series for applications requiring up to 3A output current with good efficiency and moderate switching frequency. Select XZ3xxx series for ultra-compact designs where solution size is critical and output current requirements are 2A or less. Consider efficiency, size, and cost trade-offs.
faqs.decisionGuide: XZ2xxx for high current and efficiency; XZ3xxx for minimum size and light load applications.

faqs.question: What input voltage range do Silicontent DC-DC converters support?
faqs.answer: Silicontent offers DC-DC converters with various input voltage ranges. The XZ2xxx and XZ3xxx series support 2.3V to 5.5V input, ideal for single Li-ion battery applications. Other families support wider ranges up to 60V for industrial and automotive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select converter based on your input voltage source; verify minimum and maximum input voltage requirements.

faqs.question: Do Silicontent converters support adjustable output voltage?
faqs.answer: Yes, most Silicontent DC-DC converters feature adjustable output voltage set by external resistor divider. Some products also offer fixed output voltage options (1.8V, 3.3V, 5V) in smaller packages without external resistors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use adjustable version for flexibility; fixed voltage version for smallest size and lowest BOM cost.

faqs.question: What protection features are included?
faqs.answer: Silicontent DC-DC converters include comprehensive protection: cycle-by-cycle current limit, thermal shutdown, undervoltage lockout (UVLO), and short-circuit protection. Some products also include overvoltage protection and power-good indicators.
faqs.decisionGuide: All basic protections are built-in; verify specific protection features in datasheet for your application requirements.

faqs.question: Are Silicontent DC-DC converters suitable for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Silicontent offers AEC-Q100 qualified DC-DC converters for automotive applications. These products meet automotive reliability requirements and are suitable for infotainment, ADAS, and body electronics applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Specify automotive-grade products for in-vehicle applications; verify AEC-Q100 qualification status.

categories.name: LDO Regulators
categories.description: Ultra-low noise LDO linear regulators for sensitive analog and RF applications
categories.longDescription: Silicontent LDO regulators provide clean, stable power for noise-sensitive applications such as RF transceivers, precision ADCs, and audio circuits. With ultra-low noise (<10μVrms), high PSRR (>70dB), and low dropout voltage, these LDOs deliver exceptional performance in compact packages. As an authorized Silicontent distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive selection guide to help you choose the right LDO for your noise-sensitive application.

series.name: XZ1xxx Series
series.description: Ultra-low noise LDOs for RF and precision analog applications

series.name: XZ2xxx Series
series.description: Low-power LDOs with ultra-low quiescent current for battery applications

categories.selectionGuide: Choose XZ1xxx series for noise-sensitive applications requiring ultra-low noise and high PSRR. Select XZ2xxx series for battery-powered applications requiring low quiescent current. Consider dropout voltage, noise, and PSRR requirements.

products.name: XZ1001 Ultra-Low Noise LDO
products.shortDescription: 300mA ultra-low noise LDO regulator with 8μVrms noise and 75dB PSRR for sensitive RF and analog applications
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ1001 is an ultra-low noise LDO regulator designed for noise-sensitive applications such as RF transceivers, precision ADCs, and audio circuits. With output noise of only 8μVrms and PSRR of 75dB at 1kHz, it provides exceptionally clean power.

The device operates from a 2.0V to 5.5V input and delivers up to 300mA output current. It features low dropout voltage of only 150mV at 300mA, making it efficient even with small input-output voltage differentials.

Available in compact SOT-23 and 1.5mm x 1.5mm DFN packages, the XZ1001 is ideal for space-constrained portable devices. It includes thermal shutdown and current limit protection for reliable operation.

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Analog Power
faeReview.content: The XZ1001 is my go-to LDO for noise-sensitive applications. The 8μVrms noise figure is excellent for this class of device, and the 75dB PSRR at 1kHz effectively rejects input ripple. I've used this in RF front-end designs where power supply noise directly impacts receiver sensitivity. One important tip: place the output capacitor as close as possible to the LDO pins - this minimizes noise pickup and ensures stability. The dropout voltage is reasonable at 150mV, but if you need lower dropout, consider the XZ1002 variant. Overall, a solid performer for the price point, especially for consumer electronics where noise performance is critical.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent ultra-low noise LDO with 8μVrms noise for RF applications

alternativeParts.reason: High-performance LDO with similar noise specifications
alternativeParts.useCase: Alternative for designs requiring TI compatibility

alternativeParts.reason: Low noise LDO from Analog Devices with comparable performance

companionParts.description: Buck converter for efficient step-down conversion before LDO
Lower current LDO for secondary rails

faqs.question: What is the output noise of XZ1001?
faqs.answer: The XZ1001 features ultra-low output noise of 8μVrms over the 10Hz to 100kHz bandwidth. This low noise makes it ideal for powering sensitive RF and analog circuits where power supply noise can degrade performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use XZ1001 for noise-sensitive applications; verify noise specifications meet your circuit requirements.

faqs.question: What is PSRR and why is it important?
faqs.answer: PSRR (Power Supply Rejection Ratio) measures how well the LDO rejects noise from the input supply. XZ1001 offers 75dB PSRR at 1kHz, meaning it rejects 99.98% of input ripple at this frequency. High PSRR is important when the input supply has ripple (such as from a switching converter).
faqs.decisionGuide: High PSRR is critical when using LDO after switching converters; XZ1001 provides excellent ripple rejection.

faqs.question: What dropout voltage can I expect?
faqs.answer: XZ1001 has a typical dropout voltage of 150mV at 300mA load. This means the input voltage must be at least 150mV higher than the output voltage for proper regulation. Dropout voltage decreases at lighter loads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure input voltage has sufficient headroom above output voltage; consider dropout when battery is near end of discharge.

faqs.question: What output capacitor should I use?
faqs.answer: XZ1001 is stable with ceramic capacitors of 1μF to 10μF. Use low-ESR ceramic capacitors (X5R or X7R dielectric) for best performance. Place the output capacitor as close as possible to the output pin for optimal noise performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 4.7μF ceramic capacitor for typical applications; place close to LDO pins for best performance.

faqs.question: Can XZ1001 be used after a switching converter?
faqs.answer: Yes, XZ1001 is ideal for post-regulation after switching converters. Its high PSRR effectively filters switching ripple, providing clean power to sensitive circuits. This cascaded approach combines the efficiency of switching converters with the clean output of LDOs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use XZ1001 after buck converter for noise-sensitive circuits; high PSRR provides excellent ripple filtering.

products.name: XZ1002 Low-Iq LDO
products.shortDescription: 200mA low-quiescent current LDO with 1μA Iq for extended battery life in portable and IoT applications
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ1002 is a low-power LDO regulator optimized for battery-powered applications. With ultra-low quiescent current of only 1μA, it minimizes battery drain during standby operation, extending battery life in portable devices.
The device operates from a 2.0V to 5.5V input and delivers up to 200mA output current. It features low dropout voltage and includes enable pin for power management control.
Available in compact SOT-23 and SC-70 packages, the XZ1002 is ideal for space-constrained battery-powered devices such as IoT sensors, wearables, and remote controls.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Low Power Applications
faeReview.content: The XZ1002 is specifically designed for battery-powered applications where every microamp counts. The 1μA quiescent current is exceptional - I've seen battery life extend by months in IoT sensor applications just by switching to this LDO. The trade-off is higher noise and lower PSRR compared to the XZ1001, but for digital circuits and always-on rails, this is perfectly acceptable. One design tip: use the enable pin aggressively - shut down the LDO when the subsystem isn't needed. The enable pin has very low leakage, so you won't waste current. Also, the dropout voltage is reasonable at 200mV. Overall, an excellent choice for battery-powered designs where standby current dominates battery life.
faeReview.highlight: Ultra-low 1μA quiescent current ideal for battery-powered IoT devices

alternativeParts.reason: Low-Iq LDO from TI with similar specifications
Cost-effective low-Iq LDO option

companionParts.description: Ultra-low power buck converter for main system power
Ultra-low noise LDO for sensitive circuits
Low-power battery charger for rechargeable designs

faqs.question: What is the quiescent current of XZ1002?
faqs.answer: XZ1002 has an ultra-low quiescent current of just 1μA typical. This means the LDO itself consumes only 1 microamp of current when enabled with no load, making it ideal for battery-powered applications where standby current is critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use XZ1002 for always-on rails in battery-powered devices; the 1μA Iq minimizes battery drain during standby.

faqs.question: How does XZ1002 extend battery life?
faqs.answer: XZ1002 extends battery life by consuming only 1μA during standby. In IoT devices that spend most of their time in sleep mode, this low quiescent current can mean the difference between months and years of battery life. For example, with a CR2032 battery, 1μA quiescent current allows 25+ years theoretical standby time.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate total standby current including LDO Iq; XZ1002's 1μA Iq is negligible for most battery applications.

faqs.question: What is the dropout voltage of XZ1002?
faqs.answer: XZ1002 has a typical dropout voltage of 200mV at 200mA load. At lighter loads, dropout voltage decreases proportionally. For example, at 100mA load, dropout is approximately 100mV. This allows efficient operation even as the battery voltage decreases.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure sufficient headroom between minimum input voltage and output voltage; consider dropout when battery is near end of discharge.

faqs.question: Can I use the enable pin for power management?
faqs.answer: Yes, the enable pin allows you to shut down the LDO when not needed, reducing current consumption to less than 100nA. This is useful for powering subsystems that are only active intermittently. The enable pin has very low leakage current, so it won't waste power when disabled.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use enable pin to power down unused subsystems; connect to microcontroller GPIO for software-controlled power management.

faqs.question: Is XZ1002 suitable for always-on power rails?
faqs.answer: Yes, XZ1002 is specifically designed for always-on power rails in battery-powered devices. The 1μA quiescent current makes it ideal for powering real-time clocks, standby circuits, and always-on sensors where battery life is critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use XZ1002 for always-on rails; consider XZ1001 if noise performance is more important than quiescent current.

products.name: XZ1003 Low-Noise High-Current LDO
products.shortDescription: 500mA low-noise LDO with 12μVrms noise and 70dB PSRR for high-performance analog circuits
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ1003 is a high-performance low-dropout regulator designed for demanding analog and RF applications. With 500mA output current capability and ultra-low noise of 12μVrms, it provides clean power for sensitive circuits.
The device operates from a 2.0V to 5.5V input voltage range and delivers up to 500mA output current. It features high PSRR of 70dB at 1kHz, effectively rejecting input ripple and noise.
Available in SOT-23-5 and DFN-6 packages, the XZ1003 is ideal for powering RF transceivers, precision ADCs, and high-performance processors in portable and industrial applications.

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Analog Power
faeReview.content: The XZ1003 bridges the gap between the XZ1001 and XZ1002, offering higher current capability than XZ1001 while maintaining good noise performance. The 500mA capacity is perfect for powering RF power amplifiers and small processors. The 12μVrms noise is slightly higher than XZ1001 but still excellent for most applications. The lower dropout voltage of 120mV is an improvement over XZ1001. I've used this in several camera module designs where it powers the image sensor and signal processing circuits. The high PSRR at 1kHz effectively filters switching noise from upstream converters. Overall, a versatile LDO that offers a good balance of performance and current capability.
faeReview.highlight: Versatile 500mA LDO with excellent noise and PSRR performance

alternativeParts.reason: High-current low-noise LDO from ADI

companionParts.description: Compact buck converter for auxiliary rails

faqs.question: What is the dropout voltage of XZ1003?
faqs.answer: XZ1003 has a typical dropout voltage of 120mV at 500mA load. This is lower than many competing LDOs, allowing more efficient operation from low input voltages. At lighter loads, dropout voltage decreases proportionally.
faqs.decisionGuide: The low dropout voltage extends battery life by allowing operation as battery voltage decreases; ideal for battery-powered applications.

products.name: XZ1004 Automotive LDO
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q100 qualified 300mA LDO with wide temperature range for automotive applications
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ1004 is an automotive-grade low-dropout regulator designed for in-vehicle applications. AEC-Q100 qualified and rated for -40°C to +125°C operation, it meets the stringent reliability requirements of automotive electronics.

The device operates from a 3.0V to 40V input voltage range, handling the wide voltage variations seen in automotive electrical systems. It delivers up to 300mA output current with comprehensive protection features.
Available in SOIC-8 and DFN-8 packages, the XZ1004 is ideal for automotive infotainment, ADAS, body electronics, and other in-vehicle applications requiring reliable power regulation.
features: Thermal shutdown and current limit protection

faeReview.author: Jennifer Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Automotive Applications
faeReview.content: The XZ1004 is specifically designed for the harsh automotive environment. The 3V-40V input range handles everything from cold crank to load dump conditions. I've used this in several automotive projects including infotainment systems and camera modules. The AEC-Q100 qualification gives customers confidence in reliability. The protection features are comprehensive - reverse battery, load dump, thermal shutdown - all essential for automotive. The trade-off is higher quiescent current and noise compared to consumer-grade LDOs, but this is acceptable for automotive applications. The wide temperature range ensures operation in extreme climates. Overall, a robust automotive LDO that meets industry standards.
faeReview.highlight: AEC-Q100 qualified automotive LDO with comprehensive protection features

alternativeParts.reason: Automotive LDO from TI with similar specifications

companionParts.description: Power module for simplified automotive power

faqs.question: What automotive qualifications does XZ1004 have?
faqs.answer: XZ1004 is AEC-Q100 qualified, which is the standard automotive reliability qualification. It has been tested for temperature cycling, high-temperature operating life, and other stresses relevant to automotive applications. The device is also qualified for the full automotive temperature range of -40°C to +125°C.
faqs.decisionGuide: AEC-Q100 qualification ensures automotive reliability; suitable for all in-vehicle applications.

faqs.question: How do I choose between XZ1001 and XZ1002?
faqs.answer: Choose XZ1001 for noise-sensitive applications requiring ultra-low noise (8μVrms) and high PSRR (75dB). Select XZ1002 for battery-powered applications where quiescent current (1μA) is more important than noise performance. XZ1001 is ideal for RF and analog circuits, while XZ1002 is perfect for always-on digital rails.
faqs.decisionGuide: XZ1001 for noise-sensitive RF/analog; XZ1002 for low-power battery applications.

faqs.question: What is PSRR and why does it matter?
faqs.answer: PSRR (Power Supply Rejection Ratio) indicates how well an LDO rejects noise from its input supply. Higher PSRR means better filtering of input ripple. XZ1001 offers 75dB PSRR, meaning it rejects 99.98% of input ripple at 1kHz. This is critical when using an LDO after a switching converter.
faqs.decisionGuide: High PSRR is important for post-regulation; choose XZ1001 for best PSRR performance.

faqs.question: What is dropout voltage?
faqs.answer: Dropout voltage is the minimum input-to-output voltage differential required for the LDO to maintain regulation. XZ1001 has 150mV dropout at 300mA, meaning the input must be at least 150mV higher than the output. Lower dropout allows operation with lower input voltages.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider dropout voltage when battery is near end of discharge; ensure sufficient headroom for worst-case conditions.

faqs.question: Can I use ceramic capacitors with Silicontent LDOs?
faqs.answer: Yes, Silicontent LDOs are designed to be stable with ceramic capacitors. Use X5R or X7R dielectric capacitors with values of 1μF to 10μF. Place capacitors close to the LDO pins for best performance. Ceramic capacitors provide low ESR and small size.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use ceramic capacitors for best performance; place close to LDO pins for stability and noise reduction.

faqs.question: Are Silicontent LDOs suitable for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Silicontent offers automotive-grade LDOs that are AEC-Q100 qualified. These devices meet automotive reliability requirements and are suitable for infotainment, ADAS, and body electronics applications. Contact BeiLuo Electronics for automotive product availability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Specify automotive-grade variants for in-vehicle applications; verify AEC-Q100 qualification status.

categories.name: Power Modules
categories.description: Integrated power modules with inductor for easy system integration
categories.longDescription: Silicontent power modules integrate the DC-DC converter, inductor, and passive components into a single compact package. This simplifies design, reduces time-to-market, and ensures optimal performance with minimal engineering effort. As an authorized Silicontent distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive selection guide and technical support for power module integration.

series.name: XZ3xxx Series
series.description: Integrated power modules with built-in inductor

series.name: XZ4xxx Series

categories.selectionGuide: Choose power modules when you need fastest time-to-market and simplified design. Modules include optimized inductor and compensation, eliminating component selection and layout challenges. Ideal for designs without power supply expertise.

products.name: XZ3001 3A Power Module
products.shortDescription: Fully integrated 3A power module with built-in inductor and compensation for simplified power supply design
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ3001 is a fully integrated power module that includes the DC-DC converter, optimized inductor, and compensation components in a single package. This eliminates the need for external inductor selection and complex compensation design.
The module delivers up to 3A output current from a 2.5V to 5.5V input. It features high efficiency up to 94% and requires only input and output capacitors for a complete power solution.

Available in a compact 4mm x 4mm QFN package, the XZ3001 significantly reduces design time and ensures optimal performance. It is ideal for applications where time-to-market is critical or power supply design expertise is limited.
features: Fully integrated solution with built-in inductor
Simple design - only 2 external capacitors needed

faeReview.author: Robert Li
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Systems
faeReview.content: The XZ3001 power module is a game-changer for teams without dedicated power supply engineers. The integrated inductor and compensation eliminate the most challenging aspects of DC-DC design. I've recommended this to several customers who needed to get to market quickly, and the results have been excellent. The efficiency is nearly as good as discrete designs, and the thermal performance is surprisingly good thanks to the optimized internal layout. One thing to note: the package is taller than discrete solutions due to the integrated inductor, so verify height constraints in your mechanical design. For rapid prototyping or designs where you want guaranteed performance without extensive validation, this module is an excellent choice.
faeReview.highlight: Fully integrated power module eliminates inductor selection and compensation design

alternativeParts.reason: TI power module with similar integrated approach
alternativeParts.useCase: Alternative for TI-based designs requiring module solution

alternativeParts.reason: Compact power module from MPS

companionParts.description: Discrete buck converter for cost-sensitive designs

faqs.question: What are the advantages of using a power module?
faqs.answer: Power modules offer several advantages: 1) Simplified design - no inductor selection or compensation calculation needed, 2) Faster time-to-market - guaranteed performance without extensive validation, 3) Smaller solution size - optimized integration saves board area, 4) Better reliability - factory-tested and optimized design. The trade-off is slightly higher cost compared to discrete solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use power modules for fastest time-to-market and simplified design; use discrete solutions for cost optimization.

faqs.question: What external components are needed with XZ3001?
faqs.answer: XZ3001 requires only input and output capacitors for a complete power solution. The inductor and compensation network are integrated inside the module. Typically, a 22μF input capacitor and 22μF output capacitor are sufficient. This significantly reduces BOM complexity compared to discrete designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Only 2 capacitors needed; use ceramic capacitors with X5R or X7R dielectric for best performance.

faqs.question: How does the efficiency compare to discrete solutions?
faqs.answer: XZ3001 achieves up to 94% efficiency, which is comparable to well-designed discrete solutions. The integrated inductor is optimized for the converter, ensuring good efficiency. While discrete designs can potentially achieve slightly higher peak efficiency with careful optimization, the module provides guaranteed good efficiency without design effort.
faqs.decisionGuide: Module efficiency is comparable to discrete; choose module for guaranteed performance without design effort.

faqs.question: What is the height of XZ3001 package?
faqs.answer: XZ3001 is available in a 4mm x 4mm QFN package with a height of 2.5mm. The height is greater than discrete solutions due to the integrated inductor. Verify this meets your mechanical constraints before selecting the module.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify 2.5mm height meets your mechanical constraints; consider discrete solution if lower height is required.

faqs.question: Can I adjust the output voltage of XZ3001?
faqs.answer: Yes, XZ3001 features adjustable output voltage set by external resistor divider. The output can be set from 0.8V to 3.3V. This flexibility allows the same module to be used for various voltage rail requirements in your system.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use resistor divider to set desired output voltage; calculate values using feedback reference voltage from datasheet.

products.name: XZ3002 2A Compact Power Module
products.shortDescription: Compact 2A power module with integrated inductor for space-constrained portable and wearable designs
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ3002 is a compact 2A power module designed for space-constrained applications. It integrates the DC-DC converter, inductor, and compensation components in a tiny 3mm x 3mm package.
The module operates from 2.5V to 5.5V input and delivers up to 2A output current. It features high efficiency and requires only input and output capacitors for operation.
With its ultra-compact size and simplified design, the XZ3002 is ideal for portable devices, IoT sensors, and wearables where space is at a premium.

faeReview.author: Lisa Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Portable Electronics
faeReview.content: The XZ3002 is perfect for applications where space is extremely limited. The 3mm x 3mm package is incredibly small for a complete power solution. I've used this in wearable designs where every square millimeter counts. The fixed voltage options are convenient - no need for external resistors, saving even more space. Efficiency is good at 93%, though slightly lower than the larger XZ3001 due to the smaller inductor. The 2.5MHz switching frequency keeps external components small. One consideration: the smaller package has slightly lower thermal capability, so ensure your maximum load and ambient temperature are within spec. For low-power portable devices, this module is an excellent choice.
faeReview.highlight: Ultra-compact 3mm x 3mm module ideal for space-constrained portable designs

alternativeParts.reason: Ultra-small power module from TI
Compact synchronous module from MPS
alternativeParts.useCase: Alternative compact module with similar specs

faqs.question: How small is the XZ3002 power module?
faqs.answer: XZ3002 is available in a tiny 3mm x 3mm QFN package. This ultra-compact size includes the DC-DC converter, inductor, and compensation components. The small size makes it ideal for wearable devices, IoT sensors, and other space-constrained applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use XZ3002 for minimum size requirements; verify height constraints as integrated inductor adds thickness.

faqs.question: What is the difference between XZ3001 and XZ3002?
faqs.answer: XZ3001 is a 3A power module in a 4mm x 4mm package, while XZ3002 is a 2A module in a 3mm x 3mm package. XZ3001 offers higher current capability and slightly better efficiency, while XZ3002 offers smaller size. Choose based on your current requirements and space constraints.
faqs.decisionGuide: XZ3001 for higher current (3A) and efficiency; XZ3002 for minimum size (3mm x 3mm) with 2A capability.

faqs.question: Are fixed output voltage versions available?
faqs.answer: Yes, XZ3002 is available in fixed output voltage versions (1.0V, 1.2V, 1.8V, 3.3V) that don't require external resistors. This saves additional board space and simplifies BOM. Adjustable versions are also available for flexibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use fixed voltage version for smallest size and simplest design; adjustable version for flexibility.

faqs.question: What is the thermal performance of XZ3002?
faqs.answer: XZ3002 has good thermal performance for its size, with thermal resistance of approximately 40°C/W. At 2A output with 5V input and 3.3V output, power dissipation is about 1.7W, resulting in temperature rise of approximately 68°C. Ensure adequate PCB copper area for heat dissipation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use adequate PCB copper area for heat dissipation; verify maximum junction temperature is not exceeded at full load.

faqs.question: Can XZ3002 be used in battery-powered applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, XZ3002 is well-suited for battery-powered applications. The 2.5V to 5.5V input range covers single Li-ion battery operation. The high efficiency (up to 93%) maximizes battery life. The compact size is ideal for portable devices where space and weight are critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: XZ3002 is excellent for battery-powered designs; verify efficiency at your operating points for battery life calculations.

products.name: XZ3003 1A Mini Power Module
products.shortDescription: Ultra-compact 1A power module with integrated inductor for wearable and IoT applications
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ3003 is an ultra-compact 1A power module designed for space-constrained wearable and IoT applications. It integrates the DC-DC converter, optimized inductor, and compensation in a tiny 2.5mm x 2.5mm package.
Operating from 2.3V to 5.5V input, the XZ3003 delivers up to 1A output current with high efficiency. The module features automatic power-save mode for extended battery life in portable devices.
With minimal external components required, the XZ3003 enables rapid design and smallest solution size. It is ideal for smart watches, fitness trackers, and ultra-compact IoT sensors.

faeReview.author: Sarah Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Wearable Electronics
faeReview.content: The XZ3003 is the smallest power module in our lineup, and it's perfect for wearables where space is absolutely critical. The 2.5mm x 2.5mm package is incredibly small - you can fit this almost anywhere. I've used this in smartwatch designs where PCB area is measured in square millimeters. The 1A capacity is sufficient for most wearable processors and sensors. The automatic power-save mode is essential for battery life - it keeps efficiency high even when the device is sleeping. The 3MHz switching allows use of tiny 0402 inductors inside the module. Trade-offs are slightly lower efficiency and thermal capability compared to larger modules, but for wearables, the size savings are worth it.
faeReview.highlight: Ultra-compact 2.5mm x 2.5mm module for space-critical wearable designs

alternativeParts.reason: Ultra-small power module from TI

faqs.question: How small is the XZ3003 power module?
faqs.answer: XZ3003 is available in an ultra-compact 2.5mm x 2.5mm QFN package. This is one of the smallest complete power solutions available, making it ideal for wearables and IoT devices where PCB area is extremely limited. The module height is approximately 1.8mm.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use XZ3003 for minimum size requirements; verify height and area constraints in your mechanical design.

products.name: XZ3004 5A High-Current Power Module
products.shortDescription: High-current 5A power module with integrated inductor for industrial and computing applications
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ3004 is a high-current power module designed for demanding industrial and computing applications. It delivers up to 5A output current with integrated inductor and compensation in a compact 7mm x 7mm package.

Operating from 4.5V to 18V input, the XZ3004 supports a wide range of industrial and computing power requirements. It features high efficiency and excellent thermal performance for reliable operation.
The module requires only input and output capacitors, significantly simplifying design and reducing time-to-market. It is ideal for FPGA power, processor power, and industrial control systems.

faeReview.author: Robert Li
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Systems
faeReview.content: The XZ3004 is our highest-current power module, and it's a great solution for FPGA and processor power. The 5A capacity handles most mid-range FPGAs and processors with ease. The 4.5V-18V input range is perfect for industrial 12V and 24V systems. I've used this in several industrial control designs where reliability and time-to-market were critical. The integrated solution eliminates inductor selection and compensation design - just add capacitors and you're done. Efficiency is excellent at 95%, and thermal performance is good thanks to the larger package. The 7mm x 7mm size is reasonable for the current capability. Overall, an excellent choice for high-current applications where design simplicity is valued.
faeReview.highlight: High-current 5A power module for industrial and computing applications

alternativeParts.reason: High-current power module from TI

faqs.question: What is the maximum output current of XZ3004?
faqs.answer: XZ3004 can deliver up to 5A continuous output current with proper thermal management. The module includes thermal shutdown protection to prevent damage from overheating. For reliable operation, ensure adequate PCB copper area and airflow for heat dissipation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use XZ3004 for high-current applications up to 5A; verify thermal performance under worst-case conditions.

faqs.question: What are the main advantages of power modules over discrete solutions?
faqs.answer: Power modules offer simplified design, faster time-to-market, and guaranteed performance. The integrated inductor and compensation eliminate component selection and complex calculations. Modules are factory-tested and optimized, ensuring good performance without extensive validation. The trade-off is slightly higher cost compared to discrete solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use modules for fastest time-to-market and simplified design; use discrete solutions for cost optimization and maximum flexibility.

faqs.question: How do I choose between XZ3001 and XZ3002?
faqs.answer: Choose XZ3001 for higher current requirements (up to 3A) and slightly better efficiency in a 4mm x 4mm package. Select XZ3002 for space-constrained applications requiring up to 2A in a smaller 3mm x 3mm package. Both offer simplified design with integrated inductor.
faqs.decisionGuide: XZ3001 for 3A applications; XZ3002 for minimum size with 2A capability.

faqs.question: What external components are required with power modules?
faqs.answer: Power modules require only input and output capacitors. The inductor, MOSFETs, and compensation are integrated inside the module. Typically, 22μF input and output capacitors are sufficient. This significantly reduces BOM complexity and design effort compared to discrete solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Only 2 capacitors needed; use ceramic capacitors with X5R or X7R dielectric for best performance.

faqs.question: Are power modules more reliable than discrete designs?
faqs.answer: Power modules can offer better reliability due to factory optimization and testing. The integrated design eliminates variability from external component selection and placement. However, proper thermal management and PCB layout are still important. Modules are a good choice when design resources are limited.
faqs.decisionGuide: Modules offer consistent, validated performance; discrete designs offer more flexibility but require more validation.

faqs.question: Can I use power modules for high-volume production?
faqs.answer: Yes, power modules are suitable for high-volume production. While the unit cost is higher than discrete solutions, the reduced design effort and faster time-to-market can offset this. For very high volumes (100k+ units annually), discrete designs may be more cost-effective after amortizing design costs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use modules for fast time-to-market; evaluate discrete solutions for very high volume cost optimization.

categories.name: Battery Chargers

categories.longDescription: Silicontent battery chargers provide safe, efficient charging for single-cell Li-ion and Li-polymer batteries. With integrated protection features, programmable charge currents, and compact packages, these chargers are ideal for portable devices. As an authorized Silicontent distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive selection guide for choosing the right battery charger for your application.

series.name: XZ5xxx Series
series.description: Linear battery chargers for single-cell Li-ion batteries

categories.selectionGuide: Choose XZ5xxx series chargers based on charge current requirements and package size. Higher charge currents require larger packages for thermal management. All chargers include safety features and programmable charge parameters.

products.name: XZ5001 Single-Cell Li-ion Charger
products.shortDescription: 800mA linear Li-ion battery charger with automatic recharge, thermal regulation, and comprehensive safety features
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ5001 is a complete constant-current/constant-voltage linear charger for single-cell lithium-ion batteries. It delivers up to 800mA charge current with no external MOSFET or sense resistor required.

The device includes automatic recharge, preconditioning for deeply discharged cells, and comprehensive safety features including thermal regulation and battery temperature monitoring. Charge current is programmable via external resistor.
Available in compact SOT-23-5 and DFN-8 packages, the XZ5001 is ideal for smartphones, portable media players, and other handheld devices.
features: Complete linear charger - no external MOSFET needed

faeReview.author: Kevin Wu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Battery Management
faeReview.content: The XZ5001 is a solid linear charger that I've used in many portable device designs. The integrated pass element eliminates the need for external MOSFETs, simplifying the design. The 800mA maximum charge current is sufficient for most small battery applications. I particularly like the thermal regulation feature - it automatically reduces charge current if the die temperature gets too high, preventing damage. The charge voltage accuracy of ±1% is good for maximizing battery capacity without overcharging. One tip: use the thermal regulation feature by ensuring good PCB heat sinking - this allows faster charging without overheating. The battery temperature monitoring is also important for safety - always use an NTC thermistor to disable charging at temperature extremes.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable 800mA linear charger with integrated pass element and thermal regulation

alternativeParts.reason: Popular linear charger with similar specifications
alternativeParts.useCase: Alternative for designs requiring Microchip compatibility

alternativeParts.reason: Widely used linear charger with higher current capability
alternativeParts.useCase: Alternative for higher current charging requirements

companionParts.description: Buck converter for system power from battery
Low noise LDO for sensitive circuits
Power module for complete system solution

faqs.question: What is the maximum charge current of XZ5001?
faqs.answer: XZ5001 supports charge currents up to 800mA, programmable via external resistor. The actual charge current is set by the resistor value connected to the PROG pin. Higher currents require better thermal management. For thermal-limited designs, the thermal regulation feature automatically reduces current to maintain safe temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: Set charge current based on battery capacity (typically 0.5C to 1C rate); ensure adequate thermal management for higher currents.

faqs.question: How do I set the charge current?
faqs.answer: Charge current is set by a resistor connected between PROG pin and ground. The formula is: ICHARGE = 1000V / RPROG. For example, a 2kΩ resistor sets 500mA charge current. The resistor should be 1% tolerance for accurate current setting.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate RPROG using formula; use 1% tolerance resistor for accuracy; verify thermal capability at selected current.

faqs.question: What safety features are included?
faqs.answer: XZ5001 includes comprehensive safety features: thermal regulation to prevent overheating, battery temperature monitoring via NTC thermistor, preconditioning for deeply discharged cells, automatic recharge when battery voltage drops, and reverse leakage protection to prevent battery discharge when input is removed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use NTC thermistor for temperature monitoring; thermal regulation protects against overheating; all features are automatic.

faqs.question: What is the charge voltage accuracy?
faqs.answer: XZ5001 provides ±1% charge voltage accuracy (4.158V to 4.242V). This precise regulation ensures maximum battery capacity is achieved without overcharging, which could damage the battery. The tight accuracy eliminates the need for factory calibration in most applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: ±1% accuracy is suitable for most Li-ion batteries; no calibration needed; ensures safe and complete charging.

faqs.question: How does the automatic recharge feature work?
faqs.answer: After charging completes, XZ5001 monitors battery voltage. If the voltage drops below approximately 4.05V (indicating self-discharge or load), the charger automatically restarts charging. This ensures the battery remains fully charged without manual intervention, important for devices that remain plugged in.
faqs.decisionGuide: Automatic recharge maintains full battery; feature is automatic; important for devices left connected to charger.

products.name: XZ5002 Compact Li-ion Charger
products.shortDescription: 500mA compact Li-ion charger with integrated pass element for small battery applications
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ5002 is a compact linear charger designed for small battery applications such as wearables and IoT devices. It delivers up to 500mA charge current in a tiny package.
The device includes all standard safety features including thermal regulation, automatic recharge, and preconditioning. Charge current is programmable via external resistor.
Available in ultra-small SC-70 and 1.5mm x 1.5mm DFN packages, the XZ5002 is one of the smallest complete battery chargers available, making it ideal for space-constrained designs.

faeReview.author: Amy Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Wearable Applications
faeReview.content: The XZ5002 is specifically designed for small battery applications where space is critical. The SC-70 package is tiny, and the 1.5mm x 1.5mm DFN is even smaller. I've used this in smartwatch designs where every millimeter counts. The 500mA maximum current is perfect for the small batteries used in wearables (typically 100-300mAh). The thermal regulation works well - even at 500mA, the small package stays within safe limits with minimal PCB copper. One design tip: the small package has higher thermal resistance, so don't push the current too high unless you have good heat sinking. For typical wearable batteries charging at 0.5C (100-150mA), this charger works perfectly without thermal issues.
faeReview.highlight: Ultra-compact charger ideal for wearable and IoT applications with small batteries

alternativeParts.reason: Compact linear charger from Microchip
Industry-standard compact charger from Linear Technology

companionParts.description: Ultra-compact buck converter for system power
Compact power module for complete solution

faqs.question: How small is the XZ5002 package?
faqs.answer: XZ5002 is available in SC-70-5 (2mm x 1.25mm) and ultra-small DFN-6 (1.5mm x 1.5mm) packages. These compact packages make XZ5002 one of the smallest complete battery chargers available, perfect for space-constrained wearable and IoT applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use DFN package for minimum size; SC-70 for easier assembly; both packages have same electrical performance.

faqs.question: What battery sizes is XZ5002 suitable for?
faqs.answer: XZ5002 is ideal for small Li-ion batteries typically used in wearables and IoT devices, such as 100mAh to 500mAh capacities. The 500mA maximum charge current supports 0.5C to 1C charging rates for these battery sizes. For larger batteries, consider XZ5001 with higher current capability.
faqs.decisionGuide: XZ5002 for batteries 100-500mAh; XZ5001 for larger batteries requiring faster charging.

faqs.question: What is the difference between XZ5001 and XZ5002?
faqs.answer: XZ5001 supports up to 800mA charge current in SOT-23 and DFN-8 packages, suitable for larger batteries. XZ5002 supports up to 500mA in smaller SC-70 and DFN-6 packages, ideal for space-constrained designs with smaller batteries. Both offer similar features and safety protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: XZ5001 for higher current (800mA) and larger batteries; XZ5002 for minimum size with 500mA capability.

faqs.question: Does XZ5002 have thermal regulation?
faqs.answer: Yes, XZ5002 includes thermal regulation that automatically reduces charge current if the die temperature exceeds safe limits. This protects the charger from overheating, especially important in the small packages with limited heat dissipation. The feature is automatic and requires no external components.
faqs.decisionGuide: Thermal regulation is automatic; feature protects against overheating; important for small packages.

faqs.question: Can XZ5002 be used in wearable devices?
faqs.answer: Yes, XZ5002 is specifically designed for wearable devices. The ultra-compact package, 500mA current capability (perfect for small wearable batteries), and low power consumption make it ideal for smartwatches, fitness trackers, and other wearables. The thermal regulation ensures safe operation in compact enclosures.
faqs.decisionGuide: XZ5002 is excellent for wearables; small size and appropriate current for typical wearable batteries.

products.name: XZ5003 1.5A Fast Battery Charger
products.shortDescription: 1.5A linear Li-ion battery charger with fast charging capability for smartphones and tablets
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ5003 is a high-current linear battery charger designed for fast charging of larger Li-ion batteries. It delivers up to 1.5A charge current with comprehensive safety features and automatic recharge capability.

The device operates from a 4.5V to 6.5V input voltage range and supports single-cell Li-ion and Li-polymer batteries. It features programmable charge current, battery temperature monitoring, and thermal regulation for safe operation.
Available in SOP-8 and DFN-8 packages, the XZ5003 is ideal for smartphones, tablets, power banks, and other devices with larger battery capacities requiring fast charging.

faeReview.author: Kevin Wu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Battery Management
faeReview.content: The XZ5003 is our highest-current battery charger, designed for fast charging of larger batteries. The 1.5A capability can charge a 3000mAh battery in about 2 hours, which is important for user experience in smartphones and tablets. The thermal regulation is critical at these current levels - it automatically reduces current if the IC gets too hot. I always recommend good PCB copper area for heat dissipation when using this part. The battery temperature monitoring is essential for safety - always use an NTC thermistor. The charge status and fault indicators help with system monitoring. Overall, a robust fast charger for larger battery applications.
faeReview.highlight: High-current 1.5A charger for fast charging of larger Li-ion batteries

alternativeParts.reason: High-current linear charger from Microchip

companionParts.description: High-current buck for fast charging input

faqs.question: What is the maximum charge current of XZ5003?
faqs.answer: XZ5003 supports charge currents up to 1.5A, programmable via external resistor. This high current enables fast charging of larger batteries. The actual charge current may be limited by thermal regulation to prevent overheating. For reliable operation, ensure adequate PCB copper area for heat dissipation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use XZ5003 for fast charging of larger batteries (>1000mAh); verify thermal capability at maximum current.

products.name: XZ5004 USB-C PD Battery Charger
products.shortDescription: USB-C Power Delivery compatible battery charger with 2A capability and integrated power path management
descriptionParagraphs: The XZ5004 is an advanced battery charger with USB-C Power Delivery compatibility and integrated power path management. It supports up to 2A charge current and can deliver system power while charging the battery.

The device operates from a 4.5V to 14V input voltage range, supporting USB-C PD voltage levels. It features dynamic power management, battery temperature monitoring, and comprehensive safety features.
Available in QFN-16 package, the XZ5004 is ideal for USB-C devices, laptops, and high-power portable electronics requiring advanced battery management and power path control.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - USB Power Applications
faeReview.content: The XZ5004 is our most advanced battery charger, designed for USB-C Power Delivery applications. The integrated power path management is a key feature - it allows the system to run directly from input power while charging the battery, improving efficiency and reducing heat. The USB-C PD compatibility means it can work with the latest chargers and power sources. The I2C interface allows software control of charging parameters, which is useful for optimizing charge profiles. I've used this in several USB-C hub designs and tablet applications. The 2A charge current and 3A system current capability handle most portable devices. Overall, an excellent choice for modern USB-C powered devices.
faeReview.highlight: Advanced USB-C PD charger with power path management and I2C control

alternativeParts.reason: USB-C PD charger from TI with similar features

faqs.question: What is power path management in XZ5004?
faqs.answer: Power path management allows the system to operate directly from the input power source while simultaneously charging the battery. This improves efficiency by avoiding double conversion (input→battery→system) and reduces heat generation. If input power is insufficient for both system load and charging, the device intelligently prioritizes system power and reduces charge current accordingly.
faqs.decisionGuide: Power path management improves efficiency and thermal performance; ideal for high-power USB-C applications.

faqs.question: How do I choose the right battery charger for my application?
faqs.answer: Choose based on battery capacity and charge time requirements. For batteries under 500mAh, XZ5002 with 500mA capability is sufficient. For larger batteries up to 800mAh, XZ5001 provides faster charging. Both offer similar safety features and charge accuracy.
faqs.decisionGuide: XZ5002 for small batteries (<500mAh) and space-constrained designs; XZ5001 for larger batteries requiring faster charging.

faqs.question: What is the recommended charge rate for Li-ion batteries?
faqs.answer: The recommended charge rate is typically 0.5C to 1C, where C is the battery capacity in mAh. For a 1000mAh battery, 0.5C = 500mA and 1C = 1000mA. Slower charging (0.5C) is gentler on the battery and generates less heat, while faster charging (1C) reduces charge time.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 0.5C for gentle charging and longer battery life; 1C for faster charging when time is critical.

faqs.question: What safety features are important in battery chargers?
faqs.answer: Important safety features include: thermal regulation to prevent overheating, battery temperature monitoring to disable charging at temperature extremes, overvoltage protection to prevent overcharging, and reverse leakage protection to prevent battery discharge. All Silicontent chargers include these essential safety features.
faqs.decisionGuide: All Silicontent chargers include comprehensive safety features; use NTC thermistor for temperature monitoring.

faqs.question: How accurate is the charge voltage regulation?
faqs.answer: Silicontent chargers provide ±1% charge voltage accuracy (4.158V to 4.242V for 4.2V setting). This precise regulation ensures maximum battery capacity is achieved without overcharging, which could damage the battery or create safety hazards. The tight accuracy eliminates need for calibration.
faqs.decisionGuide: ±1% accuracy ensures safe and complete charging; no calibration needed; suitable for all Li-ion batteries.

faqs.question: Can I use these chargers for Li-polymer batteries?
faqs.answer: Yes, Silicontent chargers are compatible with both Li-ion and Li-polymer batteries. Both battery types use the same charging profile (constant-current followed by constant-voltage) and have the same full-charge voltage (4.2V). The chargers automatically adjust to the battery characteristics.
faqs.decisionGuide: XZ5001 and XZ5002 work with both Li-ion and Li-polymer batteries; same charging algorithm applies.

faqs.question: What types of power management products does Silicontent Technology offer?
faqs.answer: Silicontent Technology offers high-efficiency DC-DC buck/boost converters, ultra-low noise LDO linear regulators, integrated power modules, and Li-ion battery chargers. Their products are designed for consumer electronics, industrial, and automotive applications with emphasis on high power density and small form factors. As an authorized Silicontent distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive selection guide and technical support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select product category based on your application: DC-DC for efficient step-down conversion, LDO for low-noise regulation, modules for easy integration, chargers for battery management.

faqs.question: How does Silicontent compare to other power IC manufacturers?
faqs.answer: Silicontent offers competitive performance with industry-leading power density and small package options. While international vendors may have broader portfolios, Silicontent excels in compact, high-efficiency solutions with competitive pricing and strong local support in China. Their products are particularly strong in consumer electronics and portable device applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate Silicontent for space-constrained, cost-sensitive designs requiring high performance; consider alternatives for specialized applications outside Silicontent's focus areas.

faqs.question: What is the quality and reliability of Silicontent products?
faqs.answer: Silicontent products undergo rigorous testing and quality control. They are qualified for industrial temperature ranges (-40°C to +85°C) and meet standard reliability requirements. Automotive-grade products are AEC-Q100 qualified for high-reliability applications. All products are backed by BeiLuo Electronics technical support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Specify appropriate temperature grade for your application; automotive grade for high-reliability requirements; industrial grade for general applications.

faqs.question: Does Silicontent provide design support?
faqs.answer: Yes, Silicontent provides comprehensive design support including datasheets, application notes, reference designs, and evaluation boards. Technical support is available through BeiLuo Electronics FAE team for schematic review, layout guidance, and troubleshooting. Contact us for design assistance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Utilize reference designs as starting points; engage FAE support for complex multi-rail designs or challenging applications.

faqs.question: How can I get samples and evaluation boards?
faqs.answer: Samples and evaluation boards are available through BeiLuo Electronics. Contact our sales team with your specific requirements. We maintain stock of popular products for quick sample delivery and can provide evaluation boards for testing and validation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request samples early in the design phase for evaluation; order evaluation boards for reference design validation and performance testing.
